# PRD: Phase 1 — Foundation Infrastructure

**Generated by:** Lopmon (CLM Studios)
**Date:** 2026-04-17
**Status:** ✅ Approved by Citlali (2026-04-17 evening) — Path A + per-org Claude key locked. Ready for coding agent.
**Requested by:** Citlali (resuming Phase 1 after account setup sprint)

## DECISIONS LOCKED IN

- ✅ **Architecture: Path A** — single Next.js codebase, deploy to Vercel in server mode, FastAPI service is parked (not used in MVP). Heartbeat/orchestration may be revisited later as Vercel cron or Edge Functions.
- ✅ **Claude API key storage: per-organization at signup** — stored on the `orgs` row (encrypted). Signup flow asks for the key during org creation. All Claude API calls server-side read the key from the authenticated user's `org_id`.
- ⚠️ **Database state UNVERIFIED** — Citlali recalls Milo previously reconfigured the live Supabase DB but reverted because Milo had been live-deploying from local without committing. Net result: **the migrations on disk may not match the live DB schema**. The coding agent's FIRST step must be: connect to live Supabase using credentials in `apps/web/.env.local`, dump the actual schema, compare to `supabase/migrations/`, and report findings BEFORE making any changes. Do not assume migrations have been applied. Do not assume the local migration files reflect production state.

## CRITICAL CONTEXT FOR CODING AGENT

Milo has historically deployed Edify-OS by editing locally and pushing live to Vercel WITHOUT committing to git. This means:
- The git history under-represents what's actually deployed
- The deployed Vercel build may diverge from what's in the repo at any commit
- DB state especially may be out of sync with the migrations folder
- When in doubt, treat the live deployed state as authoritative for current behavior, but the repo as authoritative for what we're building toward
- Always commit changes; never live-deploy from local in this Phase 1 work

---

## 1. Goal

Make Edify-OS execute against real infrastructure. Today the Next.js app runs on mock data, has API routes that won't function in production, and has Supabase migrations that exist on disk but never touch a real database. Phase 1 closes this gap so every downstream phase (Google integrations, grants, social, programs) has a working substrate to build on.

## 2. Why

The Phase 1 row in the roadmap reads "Foundation + Smart Directors" and assumes the substrate exists. An audit on 2026-04-17 found it doesn't:

- **Architectural conflict:** `next.config.mjs` sets `output: 'export'` (static HTML) but the codebase has 14 API routes (server-only). In production, every API call returns 404. The build succeeds because the routes are never executed at build time.
- **Supabase exists in name only:** `@supabase/ssr` and `@supabase/supabase-js` are installed and 8 migrations define the full schema (`orgs`, `users`, `team_members`, `agents`, `tasks`, `memory`, `heartbeat_logs`, `heartbeat_configs`, `integrations`). Zero API routes read or write to Supabase. All data is hardcoded mock objects.
- **Auth middleware disabled:** `apps/web/src/middleware.ts.bak` contains the session-refresh + auth-redirect logic but the `.bak` extension means Next.js doesn't load it. Result: `/dashboard` is world-readable, no enforced auth.
- **Team chat is hollow:** `POST /api/team/[slug]/chat` (the core product feature) returns mock responses. It does not call the FastAPI backend, does not call Claude, does not persist anything.
- **Backend deployment is undefined:** `services/agents/` (FastAPI) has no Dockerfile, no Fly/Railway config, no CI. It runs locally via `python -m src.main` and nothing else. Frontend points at `AGENT_SERVICE_URL` which has no production target.

Until these are fixed, no integration phase can be tested end-to-end. Phase 1 is the gate.

## 3. Architecture Decision Required (Z)

Two viable paths forward — Z must pick before this PRD becomes implementable:

### Path A — Next.js server mode + Supabase, deprecate FastAPI for MVP
- Remove `output: 'export'` from `next.config.mjs`
- Deploy on Vercel (Node serverless functions handle API routes)
- All API routes call Supabase directly (read/write tables, RLS-enforced)
- Claude API calls happen in Next.js API routes (server-side), not in FastAPI
- FastAPI service is parked — revisit if/when complex orchestration is needed (e.g., scheduled heartbeats)

**Pros:** Simplest architecture. One codebase, one deploy, one auth boundary. Vercel + Supabase is the canonical Next.js + DB stack — well-trodden, lots of patterns to copy.
**Cons:** Loses the work already in `services/agents/` (heartbeat scheduler, decision-lab orchestration). Some of that may need to be rebuilt as Vercel cron jobs or Supabase Edge Functions.

### Path B — Keep FastAPI, Next.js routes proxy to it
- Remove `output: 'export'` from `next.config.mjs` (still needed for the API proxy routes to work)
- Deploy Next.js on Vercel
- Deploy FastAPI on Fly.io or Railway (containerized)
- Next.js API routes either (a) call Supabase directly for simple CRUD, or (b) proxy to FastAPI for orchestration / Claude calls
- Auth: Supabase issues JWTs, both Next.js and FastAPI validate them

**Pros:** Preserves the heartbeat backend work already done. Python services can do things Node can't (async background tasks, ML libraries if ever needed).
**Cons:** Two deploys, two auth integrations, CORS to manage, two sets of env vars, harder to reason about. Adds operational cost (Fly/Railway pricing).

**Lopmon's recommendation:** **Path A for MVP.** The FastAPI heartbeat scheduler is real work but the rest of the FastAPI surface is duplicative — for an unfunded MVP serving early Edify clients, one Vercel deploy with Supabase is dramatically less complexity. Re-introduce FastAPI if Phase 4 social posting or Phase 5 participant analytics demand long-running background work that Vercel cron can't cover.

**Z must confirm A or B before scoping the agent prompt.** Everything below assumes Path A; if Path B, the work expands and a follow-up PRD adds the FastAPI deployment + proxy layer.

## 4. Scope (assuming Path A approved)

### In scope
1. **Switch to server mode**
   - Remove `output: 'export'` from `apps/web/next.config.mjs`
   - Verify `vercel.json` build commands still work in server mode
   - Confirm Vercel deployment auto-rebuilds and serves API routes
   - Test one trivial API route end-to-end in production

2. **Restore middleware**
   - Rename `apps/web/src/middleware.ts.bak` → `middleware.ts`
   - Verify it refreshes Supabase sessions on every request
   - Verify it redirects unauthenticated users away from `/dashboard` and other protected routes
   - Adjust the redirect map if route structure has drifted since the middleware was disabled

3. **Wire Supabase to API routes (replace mocks)**
   - For each of the 14 API routes, replace mock data with real Supabase queries:
     - `/api/admin/ai-config` — `agents` table
     - `/api/admin/members` — `team_members` table
     - `/api/admin/usage` — `tasks` + token-counting view
     - `/api/briefing` + `/api/briefing/upload` — `documents` table (may need new migration)
     - `/api/decision-lab` — orchestration TBD; for now, store scenarios + runs in a `decisions` table (may need new migration)
     - `/api/heartbeat` + `/api/heartbeat/history` — `heartbeat_configs` + `heartbeat_logs` tables
     - `/api/integrations` + `/api/integrations/callback` — `integrations` table
     - `/api/notifications` — `notifications` table (may need new migration)
     - `/api/support/chat` — Anthropic SDK call, persist to `tasks` or new `support_messages` table
     - `/api/team/[slug]/chat` — Anthropic SDK call (this is the core product), persist to `tasks` or new `conversations` + `messages` tables
     - `/api/team/[slug]/conversations` — fetch from same table
   - **Where a table is missing, write a new migration** (numbered `00009_…` and onward)
   - **Run migrations against the live Supabase project** (`https://tuirnficbifoewuijzeq.supabase.co`) — not just local
   - All queries must respect Row-Level Security (RLS) — every read/write filters by `org_id` of the authenticated user

4. **Wire Google OAuth in the UI**
   - Login page (`apps/web/src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`) currently has only email/password — add a "Sign in with Google" button
   - The Supabase project already has Google OAuth provider configured (set up 2026-04-16)
   - Implement OAuth callback handling in `apps/web/src/app/auth/callback/route.ts` (or wherever the flow lands) — exchange code for session, set Supabase cookies via `@supabase/ssr`
   - Test full sign-in → session → protected route flow

5. **Remove all mock fallbacks**
   - After API routes are wired to Supabase, delete the `getMock…()` functions and inline mock data
   - The app should hard-fail (5xx) if Supabase is unreachable, not silently return fake data — silent mock fallbacks were the reason the audit took longer than it should have

### Out of scope
- Rewriting the 6 archetype system prompts (covered by `PRD-archetype-skills.md`, separate PRD)
- Decision Lab feature work beyond persistence (covered by `PRD-decision-lab.md`)
- Heartbeat backend logic (covered by `PRD-proactive-heartbeats.md`; Phase 1 only persists configs/results, doesn't run them)
- Any Phase 2+ integrations (Google APIs beyond OAuth login, grants, social, etc.)
- Backend FastAPI deployment (only relevant if Z picks Path B)
- Marketing site / landing page changes

## 5. Acceptance Criteria

Phase 1 ships when:
1. ✅ A user can visit `https://edifyos.vercel.app`, click "Sign in with Google", land in `/dashboard`, and see their org's actual data (not mocks).
2. ✅ Sending a message in `/team/[slug]/chat` (any of the 6 archetypes) triggers a real Claude API call and the conversation persists across page reloads.
3. ✅ All 14 API routes return real data from Supabase. None contain `getMock…()` or hardcoded fixtures.
4. ✅ Hitting any protected route while logged out redirects to `/login`.
5. ✅ Vercel deploy logs show no warnings about static export incompatibility.
6. ✅ At least one row exists in every table the app reads (seed data or smoke-test inserts).

## 6. Risks & Open Questions

- ✅ **Claude API key handling — RESOLVED.** Stored per-organization on the `orgs` row, encrypted. Signup flow collects it during org creation. Server-side API routes read the key based on the authenticated user's org. Out-of-the-box `pgsodium` (Supabase Vault) handles the encryption.
- **RLS policies must be written.** Migrations create tables but if RLS is enabled (which it should be), every table needs explicit `CREATE POLICY` statements or all queries return zero rows. Coding agent's audit step (above) must confirm whether existing migrations include policies, and write any missing ones.
- **Conversation/messages schema doesn't exist.** Team chat needs new tables. Coding agent designs them as part of implementation — propose schema before writing migration.
- **Heartbeat scheduler — Path A implication noted.** FastAPI scheduler doesn't run. Heartbeats would need Vercel cron or Supabase Edge Functions. Out of Phase 1 scope; flag for Phase 4 planning when we get there.
- **Live DB state unverified — see "Database state UNVERIFIED" callout above.** This is the #1 risk. Audit step before any changes.

## 7. Estimated Effort

- Path A: ~2–3 sessions of coding agent work (one Sonnet agent, ~4–6 hours total)
- Path B: ~5–7 sessions (one Sonnet agent for Next.js + Supabase, plus FastAPI deployment + proxy layer)

- [x] Per-org Claude key handling locked in
